questions.question: Who is the narrator in 'My Childhood'?
questions.explanation: The chapter is an autobiographical account by Dr. A. P. J. Abdul Kalam, focusing on his early life.

questions.question: Where did Kalam spend his childhood?
questions.explanation: Kalam describes his experiences growing up in the small town of Rameswaram.

questions.question: What kind of family did Kalam grow up in?
questions.correct_answer: A simple, loving, and supportive family
questions.explanation: He highlights the values and support he received from his family members.

questions.question: What does Kalam remember about his childhood games?
questions.correct_answer: Playing with friends, flying kites, and exploring the surroundings
questions.explanation: He fondly recalls the joy and simplicity of his early life.

questions.question: What lessons did Kalam learn from his childhood?
questions.explanation: He attributes his early experiences to shaping his character and values.

questions.question: How did Kalam describe his school life?
questions.correct_answer: Full of learning, discipline, and encouragement
questions.explanation: He fondly remembers his teachers and the influence they had on him.

questions.question: What role did books play in Kalam’s childhood?
questions.correct_answer: They sparked curiosity and inspired him to learn
questions.explanation: Reading and learning were important aspects of his formative years.

questions.question: How did Kalam describe his friends?
questions.explanation: He recalls friendships that contributed to his happy childhood.

questions.question: What theme is highlighted in the chapter?
questions.correct_answer: Childhood shapes values, dreams, and character
questions.explanation: Kalam emphasizes how his early experiences influenced his later achievements.

questions.question: Why is the chapter titled 'My Childhood'?
questions.correct_answer: Because it focuses on the early life and experiences of Kalam
questions.explanation: It is a personal account reflecting on his memories and lessons from childhood.

notes.title: A. P. J. Abdul Kalam – Early Life
notes.content: Kalam grew up in Rameswaram, in a simple and supportive family.

notes.title: Childhood Games and Fun
notes.content: Kalam recalls playing with friends, flying kites, and exploring the town.

notes.title: School and Learning
notes.content: School life provided discipline, learning, and encouragement from teachers.

notes.title: Influence of Books
notes.content: Books inspired curiosity and a desire to learn from a young age.

notes.title: Theme
notes.content: Childhood experiences shape one’s values, dreams, and character.

facts.fact: Kalam grew up in Rameswaram, Tamil Nadu.
He belonged to a simple and loving family.
His childhood involved playful activities and friendships.
Books and school shaped his curiosity and learning habits.
